title: Caloncoba lophocarpa
taxonomy: Caloncoba lophocarpa is classified in the family Ochnaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales. Caloncoba lophocarpa is native to tropical Africa. The plant is a member of the genus Caloncoba, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Caloncoba lophocarpa is a part of the broader angiosperm classification system.
uses: Caloncoba lophocarpa has been traditionally used in various parts of Africa for its medicinal properties. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. intro: Caloncoba lophocarpa, also known as the African caloncoba or African rosewood, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, treat wounds, and protect against diseases such as malaria and tuberculosis. The bioactive constituents of Caloncoba lophocarpa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and lignans, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, Caloncoba lophocarpa is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used as a source of timber and wood resin.
